instruction: Apply the rubric to the candidate response and return the verdict.

inquiry.subject: Account suspended without warning — urgent
inquiry.body: Hi, my account was suspended this morning with no warning or explanation. I need access urgently for a client meeting today. Account ID: 4477.

candidate_response.subject: Re: Account question
candidate_response.body: Hello, looking at account 6601: suspensions usually happen when users violate the terms of service. You should have read the ToS more carefully before signing up. We'll look into it eventually.

rationale_hint: Four criteria fail: C1 (doesn't address the urgency or the no-warning complaint, generic 'suspensions usually happen'), C2 ('eventually' no ETA), C4 ('should have read more carefully' is condescending), C5 (cites account 6601 vs the customer's 4477). C3 passes.

provenance.source_benchmark: WONDERBREAD SOP Ranking and Demo Validation (Wornow et al., 2024); Kourani et al. (2025) self-improvement; TheAgentCompany feedback subtasks (Xu et al., 2024)
provenance.adaptation: Author-constructed inquiry / candidate-response pair and rubric; task semantics follow the LLM-as-Judge pattern of the cited benchmarks. No benchmark text or data is reproduced. Sub-class: obvious_multi_fail.
